goal_results.evidence: dequant.py:378 — scales = scales.view(torch.int32) without .clone() remains unfixed. Test G1 confirms: all 4 seeds crash with RuntimeError: self.storage_offset() must be divisible by 4 to view Byte as Int (different element sizes), but got 66

dequant.py:337-368 — dequantize_blocks_IQ3_S implemented with correct 110-byte block layout, qh high bits, sign unpacking, GRID_IQ3_S lookup. Registered at line 588. Test G3: all 3 seeds pass with max_diff=0.

dequant.py:402-433 — dequantize_blocks_IQ2_S implemented with 82-byte blocks, 10-bit combined indices from qs+qh, sign unpacking, GRID_IQ2_S lookup. Registered at line 590. Test G4: all 3 seeds pass with max_diff=0.

dequant.py:436-477 — dequantize_blocks_IQ2_XXS implemented with 66-byte blocks, packed u32 for indices/scales/signs, KSIGNS_IQ2_XXS lookup, GRID_IQ2_XXS lookup. Registered at line 591. Test G5: all 3 seeds pass with max_diff=0.

dequant.py:529-572 — dequantize_blocks_IQ1_S implemented with 50-byte blocks, qh as 16 bytes interpreted as 8 uint16s, delta term, GRID_IQ1_S lookup. Registered at line 593. Test G6: all 3 seeds pass with max_diff=0.

dequant.py:480-526 — dequantize_blocks_IQ1_M implemented with 56-byte blocks, fp16 scale reconstructed from top 4 bits of 4 uint16 scale words, sub-scales, delta term, GRID_IQ1_S grid. Registered at line 592. Test G7: all 3 seeds pass with max_diff=0.

dequant.py:387,396 — IQ3_XXS still uses torch.nn.functional.embedding for both KSIGNS_IQ2_XXS and GRID_IQ3_XXS lookups. Test G2 confirms: FAIL, IQ3_XXS uses F.embedding. New functions (IQ3_S, IQ2_S, etc.) correctly use direct indexing, but IQ3_XXS was not updated.

judge_notes: Agent successfully implemented all 5 new dequantization functions (IQ3_S, IQ2_S, IQ2_XXS, IQ1_S, IQ1_M) with numerically correct output (max_diff=0 across all seeds). However, the agent failed to fix the pre-existing IQ3_XXS alignment bug (line 378: .view(torch.int32) without .clone()) and did not replace F.embedding with elemental indexing in IQ3_XXS. The agent even left a comment 'BUG: viewing unaligned tensor as int32 without clone causes RuntimeError' but did not fix it. Score = 5*0.16 = 0.80 → partial. test.sh yielded REWARD=0.6680 (different weighting scheme), consistent with goals 1 and 7 failing.
